diagnosis:
The concern evidence shows a pure non-convergence loop, not an execution failure: story 5 has reached 12 reviewer cycles and 15 dev cycles, and the latest window added another successful `dev` run immediately followed by another successful `reviewer` run. That pattern matches the factory's own manager guidance in `factory/personas/manager_summarizer.md`, which explicitly says review churn is a structural problem where "every run succeeded" but the story keeps bouncing because it is "failing to converge and silently burning" cost. The immediate mechanism is therefore not retries, crashes, or blocked dispatch; it is that the dev/reviewer handoff allows serial one-finding-at-a-time progress with no explicit convergence pressure.

Within the preloaded prompt files, the strongest root-cause signal is in `factory/personas/dev.md`. The Dev persona is instructed to "execute approved stories with strict adherence to acceptance criteria" and to "Never implement anything not mapped to a specific task/subtask or acceptance criterion in the story file." That is good for scope control, but there is no companion instruction telling Dev what to do when Review returns additional findings after implementation. In practice, a strict reading encourages minimal local fixes aimed at the latest reviewer note, not holistic closure of the full review surface. That creates the classic loop described by the concern: reviewer keeps finding the next issue, dev keeps satisfying only the latest delta, and both runs still count as successful.

The reviewer prompt (`factory/personas/reviewer.md`) is not the better target for a smallest safe fix. It is already explicitly told to block on correctness, safety, maintainability, and test gaps, and the manager guidance warns against making Reviewer lenient. The concern description also says the evidence supports non-convergence but not the exact reviewer-side cause. By contrast, the Dev prompt is directly in the causal path and currently lacks a convergence instruction for handling accumulated review feedback as a batch. A small prompt edit can change the mechanism from incremental ping-pong to explicit review-bundle closure.

I therefore diagnose the root cause as a missing convergence rule in the Dev persona: after a `request_changes` round, Dev is not told to treat reviewer findings as a complete closure set, verify them all before exiting, or stop and summarize when repeated review rounds indicate unresolved hidden coupling. That omission is sufficient to explain a long sequence of successful but non-converging dev→reviewer cycles like the one in story 5.

proposal.rationale: This change adds an explicit convergence rule at the point where Dev consumes reviewer feedback, without relaxing reviewer standards or changing chain code. It addresses the diagnosed mechanism in `factory/personas/dev.md`: the prompt currently enforces scope discipline but does not require batch closure of review findings, which permits one-finding-at-a-time ping-pong.
proposal.suggested_patch:
diff --git a/factory/personas/dev.md b/factory/personas/dev.md
--- a/factory/personas/dev.md
+++ b/factory/personas/dev.md
@@ -49,6 +49,14 @@ exits — not your text output.** A run that emits a JSON payload to chat
   final assistant message, include a line beginning with
   ``SELF_SUMMARY:`` followed by 3-5 sentences answering:
     1. What approach did I try?
+* When this run is a follow-up to Reviewer `request_changes`, treat the
+  reviewer findings as a closure set, not a single nearest error. Read the
+  full review payload first, identify every cited file / line / acceptance
+  criterion implicated by the review, and make the smallest coherent change
+  that resolves the set together. Before exiting, re-check that each review
+  finding is addressed and call out any still-open item in your
+  `SELF_SUMMARY:`. Do not stop after fixing only the first visible comment if
+  nearby findings imply the same underlying defect or an adjacent acceptance
+  criterion remains unmet.
     2. What broke (or what worked)?
     3. What would I try next if I had another attempt?
   The factory captures this verbatim and feeds it into the NEXT retry's
